column8: my biggest concern in home nursing is the lack of proper medical equipment and immediate clinical support during acute emergencies. Once
column9: while managing a critical patient at home
column10: the patient faced sudden respiratory distress. Due to the absence of advanced hospital monitors and a secondary team to consult
column11: managing the situation alone was highly stressful. This is why I strongly believe that working through a registered platform that verifies households and provides emergency backup would make home nursing much safer for female professionals. │ Not yet                                  │ [Through a nursing agency Word of mouth WhatsApp groups]                                           │ 5 - Very viable  │ [Verified patient profiles and background-checked households Guaranteed and on-time payment after each shift 24/7 emergency support line during shifts]                                                                                                                                                          │ Yes — definitely           │ As a specialized ICU and critical care nurse
column12: my primary focus is always on patient safety and quality care. However
column13: home nursing in Pakistan currently lacks a proper system
column14: which makes it highly challenging for female professionals due to security issues
column16: and the absence of immediate medical backup during crises. I strongly believe that a structured
column17: registered digital platform is the need of the hour. It will not only protect nurses' rights through formal contracts and secure payments but will also elevate the standard of home healthcare across the country by providing a reliable support network. │ Yes — happy to participate │
